name: Week 4 Pull (Back/Biceps/Rear Delts)
description: Beat W3's 32 pull-up total. DB row pyramid goes to 75 (W3 PR was 70x6). Preacher curl: build from W3's humbling intro — try 20/side. Cable row: push for 130 on back right machine. Your back is stronger than it's ever been.

exercises.notes: Squeeze shoulder blades, warm up upper back
W3: 10/8/8/6 = 32 total. Goal: beat 32. Aim for 4x8 (32 total) but with more consistent sets — ideally no set lower than 7. Full ROM on every rep.
W3 pyramid: 55x10, 60x8, 65x8, 70x6 (PR). This week: 55x10, 65x8, 70x8, 75x5-6 (new PR attempt on top set). Brace hard on 75 — at this weight form really matters.
W3: 140x11, 140x12, 150x8 (back left machine). This week: 145x10, 145x10, 155x8. Write down the machine — numbers don't transfer between pulleys.
W3: 120x10 (honest), 140x8 (leaning back). This week: try 130 — that's the middle ground. Back right machine. Keep torso upright, no leaning.
W3: 50 x20/20/18. Stay at 50. Goal: 3x20 all clean. External rotate hard at the end of each rep.
W3: 42.5x12 x3, 42.5x10 on set 4. This week: 42.5 x4 sets, all 12 reps. You were there in W3 — repeat it cleanly.
W3: 25x15, 25x14, 25x12+4. Seated bent-over modification is your version now. Stay at 25, try to get 3x15 clean.
W3: 15/side worked (humbling at 10 reps). This week: try 20/side for sets 1-2. Drop back to 15 on set 3 if form breaks. Find out where 20/side lands.

tips: Pull-up goal: beat 32. Consistency matters more than one big set — 4x8 > 10/8/8/6.
DB row top set: 75x5-6 is a PR attempt. Don't sacrifice form for reps — a clean 5 beats a dirty 7.
Cable row: 130 is the target. If back right machine isn't available, note whichever you use.
Preacher curl at 20/side: it'll be humbling again. That's normal — it's a strict isolation, no cheat possible.
Hands were dead in W3 from all the pulling — if grip fails, that's fine. Lats are the actual target.
Rest 3 min between pull-ups, 2 min between rows, 75s between cable isolation
